0.q: Protein interaction analysis showed that PeCBL1 can interact with PeCIPK24, 25 and 26, and form different complexes of PeCBL–PeCIPK.
As shown in Fig. 2, PeCIPK26 interacts with PeCBL1, PeCBL4, PeCBL9 and PeCBL10.

1.q: PeGLABRA3-overexpressed Arabidopsis lines (OE1 and OE2) had a greater capacity to scavenge reactive oxygen species (ROS) ... under salinity stress.

3.q: stable overexpression of PeZFP38 in Arabidopsis thaliana increased biomass (~68% fresh weight), enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities (e.g., SOD activity reached 1.7-fold that of WT), and reduced oxidative damage (~30% MDA decrease).

4.q: The overexpression of PeMIPS1 resulted in the increased activities of antioxidant enzymes and the accumulation of ascorbate, which partly accounted for the enhanced reactive oxygen species-scavenging capacity and the lowered hydrogen peroxide and malondialdehyde levels in the transgenic poplar.

6.q: PeREM6.5-transgenic plants had reduced accumulation of Na+ due to the Na+ extrusion promoted by the H+-ATPases.

7.q: Overexpressing lines significantly improved tolerance to salt treatment and reduced reactive oxygen species production. RNA interference lines showed the opposite.

8.q: PeGRP2 overexpression in the hybrid Populus... increased its salt sensitivity, which was reflected in the plant's growth and photosynthesis... greater decrease in ETR, YII, and Fv/Fm after 10 and 15 days of salt stress

9.q: NaCl treatment (100 mM, 15 days) increased the total activity of the tested antioxidative enzymes in the WT... but significantly lower values were observed in L-7 and L-8

11.q: Protein interaction analysis showed that PeCBL1 can interact with PeCIPK24, 25 and 26, and form different complexes of PeCBL–PeCIPK.

12.q: Protein interaction analysis showed that PeCBL1 can interact with PeCIPK24, 25 and 26, and form different complexes of PeCBL–PeCIPK.

13.q: Yeast two-hybrid assay showed that P. euphratica REM6.5 interacted with RPM1-interacting protein 4 (PeRIN4).

14.q: these results suggest that PeNHX1–6 function as vacuolar Na+/H+ exchangers and that PeNHX products play an important role in the salt resistance of P. euphratica.

15.q: PeNHX3-GFP localized predominantly to the tonoplast... The results suggest that PeNHX3 localized to the tonoplast and functioned in the compartmentalization of Na+ into the vacuole.
PeNHX3 mediated Na+, K+ and Li+ transport in a yeast growth assay.

16.q: we identified and functionally characterized another component of the SOS pathway in this tree called PeSOS2 or PeCIPK26.

18.q: The ethylene response factor (ERF) family is one of the largest plant-specific transcription factor families, playing an important role. Among them, the PeERF1 gene was highly connected in the turquoise module.

20.q: the paralog of PeSOS1 (PeSOS1.2) can complement the Escherichia coli mutant EP432; the double mutant E. coli strain EP432, which has deletions in its nhaA and nhaB Na+/H+ antiporter genes, was used to do complementation experiments.

22.q: When PePLDδ-pro-LUC was co-transformed with PeGLABRA3-62-SK into tobacco leaves, a significant increase in luciferase luminescence was observed. An LRA confirmed that PeGLABRA3 activated the PePLDδ promoter.

23.q: PeWRKY1 binding to the PeHA1 W-box in the promoter region was verified by a yeast one-hybrid assay, EMSA, luciferase reporter assay, and virus-induced gene silencing.

24.q: Yeast one-hybrid assays and luciferase analysis showed that PebHLH35 can directly bind to the promoter region of PeGSTU58 and activate its expression.

25.q: We identified and functionally characterized another component of the SOS pathway in this tree called PeSOS2 or PeCIPK26.
Expression of PeSOS1 partially suppressed the salt sensitive phenotypes of the EP432 bacterial strain, which lacks the activity of the two Na+/H+ antiporters EcNhaA and EcNhaB.

26.q: PeCIPK26 conferred salt-stress tolerance through lower Na+ accumulation and a higher capacity to discharge Na+, thus protecting plants from toxic Na+ damage.

27.q: The results suggest that CBL1 interacts with CIPK24, CIPK25 and CIPK26 to regulate Na+/K+ homeostasis in Populus euphratica.

28.q: PePLDδ-overexpressed plants increased the transcription of genes encoding the plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter (AtSOS1) and H+-ATPase (AtAHA2), which enabled transgenic plants to proceed with Na+ extrusion and reduce K+ loss under salinity.

29.q: Transgenic plants exhibited three-fold higher Na+ efflux than WT and VC after 12 h of NaCl stress... transgenic plants exhibited lower K+ loss than in WT Arabidopsis and VC.

30.q: Transgenic plants exhibited three-fold higher Na+ efflux than WT and VC after 12 h of NaCl stress... transgenic plants exhibited lower K+ loss than in WT Arabidopsis and VC.

31.q: PeGLABRA3-overexpressed Arabidopsis lines (OE1 and OE2) had a greater capacity to scavenge reactive oxygen species (ROS) ... under salinity stress.

32.q: overexpressing PeNAC1 significantly increased the (1,3;1,4)-β-d-glucan content ... which suggested that PeNAC1 regulates the synthesis of (1,3;1,4)-β-d-glucan

33.q: PeREM6.5 regulated H+-ATPase activity in the PM, thus enhancing the plant capacity to maintain ionic homeostasis under salinity.

34.q: PeSTZ1 confers salt stress tolerance by scavenging the accumulation of ROS through regulating the expression of PeZAT12 and PeAPX2 in poplar.

42.q: SOS1 has undergone duplication in Populus euphratica...we discovered a new variant of the SOS1 gene, which we named SOS1.2...SOS1 and SOS1.2 have high sequence identity but differ in the 5′ coding region and the 3′ region.

43.q: the paralog of PeSOS1 (PeSOS1.2) can complement the Escherichia coli mutant EP432; SOS1 has undergone duplication in Populus euphratica

46.q: conferring Na+ and ROS homeostasis control via signaling pathways mediated by PLDδ and phosphatidic acid.

47.q: Expression of PeSOS1 partially suppressed the salt sensitive phenotypes of the EP432 bacterial strain, which lacks the activity of the two Na+/H+ antiporters EcNhaA and EcNhaB.

48.q: we were able to identify the pathway that is regulated by CBL–CIPK under low K? and salt stress in Populus.

50.q: PePLDδ-derived PA enhanced the salinity tolerance by regulating ROS and K+/Na+ homeostasis in Arabidopsis

51.q: During the de novo synthesis of MI, MIPS is the key rate-limiting enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of D-glucose-6-phosphate to myo-inositol-3-phosphate.

52.q: the silencing of PeGLABRA3 led to a decrease in PePLDδ under salt conditions.

53.q: salt stress up-regulated PeHA1 transcription due to the binding of PeWRKY1 to the W-box in the promoter region of PeHA1.

54.q: we confirmed that a SALT TOLERANCE ZINC FINGER1 transcription factor from Populus euphratica (PeSTZ1) could regulate the expression of PeZAT12 by dual-luciferase reporter (DLR) assay and electrophoretic mobility shift assay.

55.q: we confirmed that a SALT TOLERANCE ZINC FINGER1 transcription factor from Populus euphratica (PeSTZ1) could regulate the expression of PeZAT12 by dual-luciferase reporter (DLR) assay and electrophoretic mobility shift assay.

56.q: ASCORBATE PEROXIDASE2 (PeAPX2) can be activated by PeZAT12 and PeSTZ1, promoting the accumulation of cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase (APX) to scavenge reactive oxygen species (ROS) under salt stress.
